source_metadata.title_raw: James R. Sutton - I-19-165 - August 27, 2010 - San Francisco

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
James R. Sutton 
150 Post St. Suite 405 
San Francisco, CA 94108 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-19-165 
Dear Mr. Sutton: 
August 27, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of your numerous political 
committee clients regarding the campaign advertising provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act"). 1 Because your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informal 
assistance. 2 
QUESTION 
What are the background color, word centering, printed box and line height disclosure 
requirements for large print campaign advertisements paid for by committees other than political 
party committees and candidates' elective office committees under Section 84504.2? 
CONCLUSION 
Section 84504.2 as applicable to large print campaign advertisements requires a disclosure 
to have a solid contrasting background color, with a text line height of five percent of the height of 
the advertisement per line. The background color is not required to be white, there is no "box" 
requirement, and no centering requirement. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires certain communications qualifying as "advertisements" under Section 
84501 to include disclosures. Generally, there are three separate disclosures that may be required on 
an advertisement: 
(1) "Ad paid for by" or "Paid for by" followed by the committee name (Section 
84502), 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


File No. I-19-165 
Page No. 2 
(2) "committee major funding from" followed by the names of top contributors 
(Section 84503), and 
(3) whether the advertisement was authorized or not authorized by a candidate or 
committee controlled by a candidate. (Section 84506.5.) 
The inclusion of each type of advertisement disclosure, as well as the disclosure's content 
and formatting, varies greatly depending on the type of media used, type of committee paying, and 
if it is an independent expenditure. These particular requirements were enacted into law in 2017 by 
the Disclose Act (AB 249). 
Your question regards the print advertisement disclosure requirements set forth in Section 
84504.2, particular to large print advertisements such as billboards and yard signs, paid for by a 
committee other than a political party committee or the candidate's elective office committee. 
Section 84504.2, subdivision (a) requires that print advertisements must include the disclosures 
required by Sections 84502, 84503, and 84506.5. The section then provides formatting 
requirements in subdivision (a), items (1) through (6): ·. 
(1) The disclosure area shall have a solid white background and shall be in a 
printed or drawn box on the bottom of at least one page that is set apart from any 
other printed matter. All text in the disclosure area shall be in contrasting color 
and centered horizontally in the disclosure area. 
(2) The text shall be in an Arial equivalent type with a type size of at least 10 
point for printed advertisements designed to be individually distributed, including, 
but not limited to, ma~lers, flyers, and door hangers. 
(3) The top of the disclosure area shall include the disclosure required by Sections 
84502 and 84503. The text of the disclosure shall be underlined ifthere are any 
top contributors. 
(4) The top contributors, if any, shall each be disclosed on a separate horizontal 
line separate from any other text, in descending order, beginning with the top 
contributor who made the largest cumulative contributions on the first line. The 
name of each of the top contributors shall be centered horizontally in the 
disclosure area and shall not be underlined. The names of the top contributors 
shall not be printed in a type that is condensed to be narrower than a normal non-
condensed Arial equivalent type·. 
(5) A committee subject to Section 84506.5 shall include the disclosure required 
by Section 84506.5, which shall be underlined and on a separate line below any of 
the top contributors. 
(6) A committee subject to Section 84223 shall next include the text "Funding 
Details At [insert Commission Internet Web site]," which shall be underlined and 
printed on a line separate from any other text. 


File No. 1-19-165 
Page No. 3 
As you note, Section 84504.2, subdivision (b) has specific format requirements for print 
advertisements larger than those designed to be individually distributed, such as billboards and yard 
signs, regarding the disclosure line height, background color, and allowance for horizontal line 
adjustments. Subdivision (b) states: 
(b) Notwithstanding paragraphs (2) and (4) of subdivision (a), the disclosures 
required by Sections 84502, 84503, and 84506.5 on a printed advertisement that is 
larger than those designed to be individually distributed, including, but not limited 
to, yard signs or billboards, shall be in Arial equivalent type with a total height of 
at least 5 percent of the height of the advertisement, and printed on a solid back-
ground with sufficient contrast that is easily readable by the average viewer. The 
text may be adjusted so it does not ·appear on separate horizontal lines, with the 
top contributors separated by a comma. 
While subdivision (b) provides the specific requirements for large print advertisements, it 
contains a reference to exclude certain items in subdivision (a), but not others. This creates some 
ambiguity. Charged with the duty of the implementation and effective administration of the Act, as 
well as providing the regulated community with compliance assistance, our reading of the statute 
follows the established rule of statutory construction that particular provisions prevail over general 
provisions. Our reading of Section 84504.2 requirergents for large advertisements is explained 
below. 
Section 84504.2(b) states. the disclosure shall be "printed on a solid background with 
sufficient contrast that is easily readable." This language is controlling because it is a more 
particular provision to large print advertisements than Section 84504.2(a)(l ). Additionally, the 
disclosure text does not need to be centered due to the specific language in Section 84504.2(b ), 
which states "[t]he text may be adjusted so it does not appear on separate horizontal lines, with the 
top contributors separated by a comma." Therefore, for large print advertisement disclosures that 
fall under Section 84504.2(b ), there is no requirement that the background be white, or for a printed 
box around the disclosure. A background that is solid in color with sufficient contrast that is easily 
readable is required. The text need not be centered. The Commission staff flagged this issue as an 
item for technical clean-up legislation in Assembly Bill 864.3 This bill is authored by Assembly 
member Mullin, the author of the Disclose Act, and currently before the Legislature. 4 Until such 
time as the Legislature provides statutory clarification, the Commission does not require the large 
print advertisement disclosure text to be centered, appear inside a box, or that the background be 
white in color. 
With regard to the Section 84504.2(b) five percent height requirement for disclosures, you 
question Commission staff's interpretation that each disclosure required under Section 84502, 
84503, or 84506.5 must have a line height of at least five percent of the advertisement. Subdivision 
(b) states, "the disclosures required by Sections 84502, 84503, and 84506.5 ... shall be in Arial 
equivalent type with a total height of at least 5 percent of the height of the advertisement .... " 
3 See the Commission's agenda and minutes for the November and December 2018 meetings. 
http:/ /w\ w. fopc.ca.gov/about-fppc/heari ngs-meet in g,s-workshops/cu1Te111-agenda/past-agendas/20 18-agendas.htm I 
4 http://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=201920200AB864 
, 


File No. I-19-165 
Page No. 4 
General rules of grammar establish that a modifier should be located closest to the word it is 
modifying. In this case, the specified font, "Arial equivalent type," is immediately followed by the 
phrase "total height of at least 5 percent of the height of the advertisement" requirement. 
Accordingly, "5 percent of the height" modifies the specified font, not "the disclosures." Similarly, 
"disclosures" is plural because there are multiple disclosures potentially required on an 
advertisement, so the five percent height requirement applies to each line of the disclosures, not all 
the disclosures in totality. 
This interpretation of Section 84504.2(b) is consistent with the wider context of the statute's 
enactment. The purpose of this provision is to ensure that the required disclosures are each 
displayed in a large enough font to be read by a viewer. As part of implementing the Disclose Act, 
Commission staff consulted with the bill author's office regarding the intent of this language. The 
author's office indicated that it was their intent that the "5 percent" language would apply to each 
line of the disclosures, consistent with the Commission's longstanding interpretation of a similar 
provision in prior law. Interpreting the "5 percent of the height of the advertisement" to apply to the 
disclosures as a whole, would reduce the font on each line below five percent when more than one 
disclosure line is required. For example, the font on three lines, under this interpretation, would 
reduce to an Arial type height of approximately 1.6 percent of the advertisement. This would result 
in readability issues and defeat the purpose of the disclosure requirements. Therefore, each line of 
the disclosure must be at least five percent of the height of the advertisement. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
LKH:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
~t:¾/4 
L. Karen Harrison 
CJ'" 
Counsel, Legal :Oivision

sections.question:
What are the background color, word centering, printed box and line height disclosure 
requirements for large print campaign advertisements paid for by committees other than political 
party committees and candidates' elective office committees under Section 84504.2?
sections.conclusion:
Section 84504.2 as applicable to large print campaign advertisements requires a disclosure 
to have a solid contrasting background color, with a text line height of five percent of the height of 
the advertisement per line. The background color is not required to be white, there is no "box" 
requirement, and no centering requirement.
